subject: Re: M species creation crashes Windows - training denoising model
body:
Hi Dennis,

Wow, a reboot isn't easy to cause! Is that 1.0.9 or 1.1.0? What GPU(s) are 
you using? Training is quite memory-heavy, though mostly not because of the 
half-maps but because of the intermediate results the neural net needs to 
keep for gradient calculation.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, September 27, 2022 at 3:34:15 PM UTC-7 dennisj...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hello,
>
> During the species creation in M, steps 1,2, and 3 out of 5 go well and 
> after a few seconds at 4/5, the PC hard crashes. The screen goes black and 
> starts a boot up again. It looks like the crash happens such that no error 
> logs are created by the system and only after boot up does it recognize 
> something went wrong.
>
> Is it possible that the step that trains the denoising model could 
> completely fill the GPU memory? I thought the training only uses two 
> refined half-maps and not individual particles themselves. That's the only 
> thing I can think of that might cause the crash.
>
> Best,
> Dennis
>
